last_item: install/provision — InstallPreflight's two FakeRunner-driven clobber guards (internal/pg/preflight.go) had ZERO tests: portListening (refuses install when something is bound to 5432) and listClusters (refuses when an existing Debian cluster is present). Both must FAIL CLOSED — a probe that can't run must surface an error so preflight aborts, never a silent 'clean host' that lets Provision overwrite a live datadir (band-2 preflight, Mode-A prove-it).
last_result: SHIPPED (test only, no bug): new internal/pg/preflight_test.go (8 tests) driving the real methods via &Manager{runner: FakeRunner}. portListening: detects 127.0.0.1:5432 + IPv6/wildcard ([::]:5432,*:5432,[::1]:5432); reads free on near-miss-only rows; FAILS CLOSED (returns error, busy=false) on ss probe error, asserting the port is named. listClusters: parses columns row-for-row incl. dotted 17.2->17; skips blank/short(<6 fields)/non-numeric-Ver lines while keeping the valid one; FAILS CLOSED on pg_lsclusters error (nil clusters + error, never swallowed). No bug — both guards correct; tests lock the contract. The FS-reading InstallPreflight checks (existingInstallClusters dir-scan fallback, detectOSCodename [pure parser already tested], freeBytes) are intentionally out of scope — need FS seams before deterministic unit testing; backlog item narrowed to that residual. Mutation-proven over 8 mutations (perl apply + git checkout, tree clean each): swallow ss err->false,nil; needle ':'+port -> port; HasSuffix->HasPrefix; HasSuffix(token)->Contains(line) [test-skeptic gap, closed by adding interior-substring near-miss rows :54321 and [fe80::5432]:22]; swallow pg_lsclusters err->nil,nil; drop major==0 skip; drop len<6 skip; cross-wire Port fields[2]->fields[3]. code-reviewer clean (matches correct, deterministic, non-tautological, matches sibling conventions). test-skeptic found the Contains(line) escape; VERIFIED as a genuine gap (case a) and CLOSED before commit (re-mutation-tested: now RED). Also analyzed the band-2 alert handleTestAlertChannel nil-deref item: it's UNREACHABLE (ch.Kind==req.Kind in {pushover,webhook}; switch always assigns notifier) — annotated in backlog for next iteration to drop-or-prove. Backend gate green: gofmt clean, vet OK, go test ./... all pass, static CGO_ENABLED=0 build OK. Web untouched. e2e N/A (pure unit + Docker unavailable).
notes: Never-ending hardening loop. Bias: (A) prove vibe-coded code actually does what it claims with tests that fail if behavior breaks; (P) preflight every risky/destructive/external op and fail loud+actionable BEFORE acting; (D) tighten defaults + self-heal so a bad config can't wedge Postgres. Re-seed the backlog via a parallel audit panel (Mode S) when it runs thin — this loop does NOT self-complete. Shared defaults live in scripts/ralph/DEFAULTS.md; real sm behavior at /primary01/git/server-management/src/sm/. Run go/make outside the sandbox if snap-confine blocks it.
north_star: An indie hacker runs a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI on best defaults — never loses data, never gets wedged, never confused. Every risky path is preflighted, every failure is loud and actionable, every claim is covered by a test that would fail if the behavior broke.
